Recovering singer Celine Dion is reportedly set to be joined by pop star Lady Gaga for her one-song performance at the ParisOympicsopening ceremony on Friday night.

French journalist Thierry Moreau shared toXthe plans of the 56-year-old singer — who suffers from Stiff Person Syndrome — less than 48 hours out from her return to the stage.

Notably, the French Canadian singer will not be belting out famous hits like My Heart Will Go On, and has instead opted for a more fitting tribute to the Games.

“Evening news: 🚨 @Celine Dion And @Lady Gaga rehearsed ‘La vie en rose’ by Edith Piaf as a duet,” the post read, translated to English.

“Dion will be dressed by Dior with a pink and black feather cape…”

The signature French tune was released in 1947 and is understood to mean “life in pink”, or more poetically, “life in rosy hues”.

Performed and covered by numerous musicians since it became a global classic, Lady Gaga offered her take on the tune in the 2018 film A Star is Born starring alongside Bradley Cooper.

TMZ reported earlier in the week that Dion would be receiving a staggering sum of $US2 million ($3 million) for belting out just one song at the event.

It is reported the singer’s extensive travel bills aboard jets, boats, and other miscellaneous expenses are also set to be covered by Olympic organisers.

Dion shared her crippling SPS diagnosis with fans in 2022, and shed light on her painful plight in the 2024 documentary I am: Celine Dion available on Prime Video.

The afflicted artist detailed her daily battle with the rare, progressive neurological disorder that causes muscular rigidity and spasms.

Olympics
title: Celine Dion reportedly set to perform La vie en rose at Paris Games opening ceremony in duet with Lady Gaga | PerthNow
description: Recovering singer Celine Dion is set to be joined by a massive talent for her one-song performance at the Paris Olympics opening ceremony. And her pay will shock you!
